Fear and Greed is at
28
(Fear).
Bitcoin has been largely flat since yesterday's session,
consolidating just above
$63K
after last week's short-squeeze recovery from the $58K low.

The quiet price action masks a busy 24 hours underneath. A
governance attack just emptied a DAO treasury. Strategy sold
Bitcoin for the second time in weeks. And two separate firms kept
buying ETH and BTC into the weakness.

Let's dive in.

Latest News
-----------

Strategy Just Sold Bitcoin Again to Pay Dividends

Strategy sold 3,588 BTC between June 29 and July 5, raising
approximately $216 million under a board-approved Bitcoin
Monetization Program. The sale came with BTC trading below the
company's average acquisition cost of $75,476.

The program authorises up to $1.25 billion in BTC sales for
dividends, reserve building, and share buybacks.

What are the three permitted uses of Strategy's Bitcoin
Monetization Program?

Read More  
( https://link.coinmarketcap.com/f/a/DYroMtJtD3GuLQu7h4PHDw~~/AAQRxRA~/v7UbnjNkBJwKKnir5hQabxNitYEaNL19pM3c2Fhq6V71ykE2qstV_6pvHyNW5INrXgDpyejkl_jkugR3p0pzsHUhpgBT1R-niI2LPB3mB6cLZne0fi395VeUAqy48mUDpuOX7wD1NUExT7d3ETdECjxJNNI3dG8_KvTRm1uEdyA~ )
   BitMine Now Controls Nearly Five Percent of All Ethereum

BitMine purchased approximately $74 million in ETH during the
week ending June 28, bringing total holdings to 5.74 million ETH
worth nearly $10 billion.

Chairman Tom Le

75577.subject: В«KoЕЎiceВ»: Syntax вҖ“ Business Operation Manager СӮР° С–РҪСҲС–
75577.body:

75575.subject: The US wants a cut of AI companies
75575.body:
----------
######                  [Collaborate with us](https://utm.genai.works/r/he2t9cz)


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8776fa6e-e2d0-4aec-9648-1bed6de3a659/GenAI_Daily_Banner.jpg?t=1778687445)
Caption: 


----------

----------
**Welcome back!** Trump says the AI giants owe the public a "contribution" and won't say what that means. A developer found a loophole that cuts Claude bills by 70%. Anthropic signed a lease on 20 years of electricity, and China ordered its own companies to delete their AI companions before a new law hits. Two governments, one week, two very different grabs.



**In today's Generative AI Newsletter:**

* **Government:** What does Washington actually want from the AI giants?

* **Claude:** How did one developer cut Claude bills by 70%?

* **Anthropic:** Why did Anthropic commit itself to the year 2046?

* **China:** What made ByteDance and Alibaba delete their AI companions?


----------



----------
## [The US wants a cut of your favorite AI](https://www.scmp.com/news/us/article/3359646/trump-hints-public-contribution-us-ai-firms)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3e5cea46-2535-46c1-a9fb-9f2eb36203f2/Beehiiv_01_-_Jul_7.png?t=1783417028)
Caption: 

The government helped make AI powerful. Now it wants to get paid.

Trump said in the Oval Office yesterday that the AI companies "making tremendous amounts of money" will be making "a contribution to the people of our country."В 

He turned to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, said "you know what I'm talking about," and declined to spell out what the contribution is.

He didn't have to.В 

This is the same government that banned Anthropic's Fable 5 for three weeks last month and floated taking a 5% stake in OpenAI. It talks about AI money like it's owed a tithe.

Nobody knows the form yet. A public stake, a national fund, a fee, a hand on the dividend. Bessent knows, apparently, and isn't saying.

A year ago Washington wanted to regulate AI. Now it wants to own a piece. Once the Treasury holds shares in the companies it's meant to police, every rule it writes comes with a stake attached.


----------











----------
## [A developer found a 70% discount inside Claude's bill](https://winbuzzer.com/2026/07/05/pxpipe-proxy-claims-fable-cost-cuts-through-code-images-xcxwbn/)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/64fb82cd-eee2-4a3b-a1cf-b7d89ed35a1e/Beehiiv_02_-_Jul_7.png?t=1783417054)
Caption: 

Anthropic charges a premium for Claude. A developer just found the loophole in its own price list.

The tool is called pxpipe, free and open-source. It sits between you and Claude Code and turns the bulky stuff, your
